reviews.review_title: Finally, a VR experience done right!
reviews.review_description:
Combines comfort, customisation & utility for an engaging experience that manages to be both familiar and innovative. Before this I was reluctant to even demo VR to elder friends but with the direction Alcove is going I'm now considering getting my 82 year old MD-suffering Mother her own headset :-) Well done Alcove VR /  AARP Innovation Labs, looking forward to future developments!

PS: reserving one star for full release :-)

The is a beautifully done app and what Oculus should’ve considered for Oculus rooms. You can move about the space easily and even turn around with just the use of the controller. I love that other apps and experiences are placed in each room as objects in the room like the toy tour bus that leads to virtual tours and the posters on the walls that lead to other VR experiences. 

I know this is fairly knee but there are a few things I’d like to see. I wish this would allow you to play your own media, this would be an awesome space to allow friends to meet and play board games. I hope that future versions allow even more customizations of the complete house. The potential in great here. I can see people willing to buy Ikea inspired book cases for their virtual darlings. I also hope that other apps are made available through nicknacks that you can place where you want. 

I love it so far and can’t wait for future updates.

A relaxing place to hang out. I like to relax here and pretend the house is mine, haha. I love the backgrounds, especially the mountain one, but it would be nice if more ever get added. A night option would also be nice. Overall, I like it a lot.

I dont have a oculus go yet but i plan on getting one very soon but just by the look of it and reading the reviews its already a 5 star. cant wait to check this out and if the dev reply to the comments hopefully they make this available for the gear vr and the s6 series thats if it isn't already.

It’s as if the team at Alcove had a crystal ball to conceive, develop and launch this app before the world turned crazy. Social isolation, particularly for the seniors generation has always been a massive issue which was why I understand Alcove was created, but who could have foreseen a need for something like this on such a scale? It also serves to show the power of VR and how it can bring people together when critics said the medium disconnected people. This is an isolation and lock-down buster powered by VR that can connect people on a level we couldn’t have imagined. Great work.
Good improvements.  The bus tour films still buffer and freeze up on me, even though I have high speed internet.  I even downloaded them vs. streaming, still no luck.

It is a beautifully designed environment. My wife and I could not get a game of checkers to work, and the video experiences load really slow, stop and start frequently. We have good high speed service.

developer_response.body:
Dear penguinchicken, we are glad to head that you're enjoying the environment, but sorry to hear about the issue with checkers. 
Could it be that either you or your wife might have been in Follow mode at the time? That feature is designed to allow a user to tag along and 'travel' through the the house and into 360 experiences seamlessly without using their own controller, but will disable the ability to play games like checkers, that require the controller.. A notification should be visible when attempting to do that, and the user who is following should be able to exit follow mode at any time. Either way, a simple app restart should fix any issues. Is the issue persisting even after restart?
If you could please provide us with more details, we will look into it asap.
Many thanks,
Alcove

reviews.review_description: This is a supercool hub to launch content or even just hang out. However, lack of free movement is a huge downside!

It’s like sitting in your own home, but it’s better..  I had to revise this review.  They deserve 13 STARS.. This app has greatly improved  throughout the months.  The videos here are absolutely "Breathtaking"... you'd have to see it for yourself.  They've added so many new features to this app that I can't simply keep up.  OMG.. there's so much involved in this app. 3D videos that captures you right in.  Games galore.. health videos, exercise videos...travel videos.. you name it.. They've got it..
I found this to be exiting and very beautiful. This helps me relax!
Simply the best app on the Go! Amazing tour bus trips in 360° 3D around the world, hot air balloon rides, road trip, underwater adventures.... and more... all in one app! 6 stars.

reviews.review_title: Awesome app,still some kinks to work out
reviews.review_description:
This is great work guys. Here are a few thoughts
-We really want to be able to take tours with our friends! It would be nice to see your friend while traveling in the game, otherwise the experience feels lonely and solitary
-It would be nice to see more video content or be able to at least pull up a web browser
-Please improve the sound design as it detracts from the experience. For example, find better more believable atmospheric sounds. Have a friends voice sound echoey when further away or in another room. 
-I had some issues getting a friend to join the game initially, as the friends list appeared empty even though my friend was in my party and added as a friend. After a few tries and 10 minutes of tinkering his name appeared in the list.
-Please keep going, this is great! Just try and make sure you can remain with your friend through the experiences and make the environments sound more believable, and maybe provide some more varied watchable content or the option of a web browser.

I really love everything about this app. I love the bus tours and the whole travel section. Love the meditation too. The games were a little basic and there’s room for improvement here. The app is however very glitchy. Please fix so I can enjoy it throughly.
The app has a nice living room you can customize with pictures in the frames. I enjoy this. It needs some updates and I look forward to it.

Absolutely stunning realistic graphics... like a picture.  Very good video app launch area.  Home pics will be awesome when updated (it's in beta).  It's just an amazing place to hang out.  I cant wait to be there with friends.  Backgrounds outside are incredible as well.  10 out of 10, developers.
Perfect way to take the VR plunge! Especially love the health and wellness activities and games! Brain games are neat, too. Thank you :)

reviews.review_title: Best social app for all ages
reviews.review_description:
I tried this app to test it out to see if it would be a good fit with my coworkers. Personally, it is lacking a few things but overall the app is very well designed and I think other apps should follow their lead.

Pros
First, the tutorial is great. I haven't seen a social app have such a nice tutorial that you can skip and go back to freely.  Second, the environment is very well designed. Third, the ideas of having mini-games in it is wonderful.

Cons
First, it's currently only available for the Go, which has very recently been discontinued. I hope that they open their virtual doors to the other current headsets/devices as well soon. Second, the mini-games does need some work as it can be a little buggy from time to time. Third, I would like to see the TV/video section expanded to a remote desktop capability so that I can show movies on the screen from my computer remotely versus being limited to AARP videos. Lastly, the rooms only allow a small amount of people and I would like an option to have many. Obviously there are limitations to this but these suggestions are available in other apps, however, this one has more potential to be wonderful in my eyes.

reviews.review_title: Love it but it needs a manual
reviews.review_description: Love this app!  Lots of VR content all in one place, but I have "how to" questions and no way to get answers.  Someone mentioned "follow mode" which would be perfect for my brother with Down Syndrome but I need to know how it works and how to turn it on. Also, I understand that photos  on the wall can be changed but can't find any info on how to change them. I understand that one can invite friends in -- but I don't see anything in the app that allows me to find people to friend. So... a manual would be helpful!

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much, Rattley! The Follow mode is easy to initiate once both users are in their headsets, in the same session in Alcove. When pointing towards an avatar, an option pops up - as a button that you can click on: "Follow". Once you select that, you are following the other user around the house, and into 360 immersive videos. The controller is disabled, until the follower disables the function (in the same way they originally enabled it), or until the session ends. 

The option would indeed be great for your brother, but you may need to start the session on both headsets yourself, go though the invite process, set his into Follow mode, and then hand him the headset. We definitely hope to make this process completely seamless end to end, but it will take some more time and further development. As we are only in Beta, we will be working toward that! 

Also as being in Beta, Alcove is constantly evolving. A full manual is not published yet, however, our team is here for you, should you have additional questions, please reach out to us directly at feedback@alcovevr.com and we will get back to you as soon as possible!

reviews.review_description: I really like the house and living room concept. It makes that starting point feel like "coming-home" to relax. I would love to see more family content options.  Have you thought about board games for the kids to play with grand parents?
I loved the environment can’t wait till it’s complete , me being a woman of age I thought the environment was completely relaxing .
